comparisons-and-truth-values.label: Comparisons and Truth Values
comparisons-and-truth-values.summary: Use comparison operators and understand True and False results.

comparison-operators.title: Which operator checks for equality?
comparison-operators.prompt: Select the operator that checks if two values are equal.
comparison-operators.hint: Think about how you would ask if two things are the same.

help.concept: The equality operator is used to compare two values to see if they are the same.
help.hint_2: It starts with an equals sign.

truthiness-in-python.title: Which of the following are considered False in Python?
truthiness-in-python.prompt: Select all values that evaluate to False.
truthiness-in-python.hint: Consider what values might not hold any weight.

help.concept: In Python, certain values are inherently considered False, such as zero and empty collections.
help.hint_1: Think about empty lists and zero.
help.hint_2: Remember, not all values are True.

fill-in-blank-true-false.title: Fill in the blank: An empty string evaluates to ____ in Python.
fill-in-blank-true-false.prompt: Choose the best value for the missing blank in the statement.
fill-in-blank-true-false.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind fill in the blank: an empty string evaluates to ____ in python..
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

compare-numbers.title: Compare Two Numbers
compare-numbers.prompt: Write a program that compares two numbers and prints whether the first is greater than, less than, or equal to the second.
compare-numbers.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

compare-numbers.starterCode:
def compare_numbers(a, b):
    # Your code here

boolean-logic.title: Boolean Logic Evaluation
boolean-logic.prompt: Create a program that evaluates a boolean expression and prints the result.
boolean-logic.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

intro-to-comparisons.title: Introduction to Comparisons
intro-to-comparisons.bodyMarkdown: In Python, comparisons are made using operators like `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, and `>=`. These operators help determine the relationship between values.

truth-values.title: Understanding Truth Values
truth-values.bodyMarkdown: In Python, every value has a truth value. For example, non-zero numbers and non-empty collections are considered `True`, while zero and empty collections are considered `False`.
